text: Research on the circular economy (CE) is essential to support and accelerate the transition to a more sustainable future and achieve climate change resilience. Current scholarly research focuses heavily on the Global North and often disregards the specific needs, barriers and opportunities of developing countries including Africa. This paper therefore initiates a continental CE research agenda, lists existing knowledge clusters and research gaps and specifies barriers to CE-related research in African countries. The methodology applies a scoping review on a total of 275 articles in English and French sourced from Google Scholar and qualitative and quantitative surveys among 38 companies in Africa managing their own CE-related projects. This research identifies South Africa as being at the forefront of CE-related research and publications on the continent. Due to the large number of publications in the country as well as on the continent related to CE in general and material loops and product reuse in particular, our study recommends that CE-related research must be carried out for individual countries on the continent to help address gaps in knowledge gaps, and barriers for each country specifically, because the majority of literature reviews concerning CE developments in Africa treat Africa as a single economy. While research has started exploring the application of business models addressing material loops and the reuse of products, the three cornerstone principles of the CE have not yet been comprehensively addressed in research related to African countries. In conclusion, this study compiles a specific list of research priorities to initiate further scholarly research and policy activities related to the development of the CE on the continent. This paper therefore represents the first attempt to conduct a review of a CE-related research agenda for countries in Africa.
